page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-144-lssd is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 144 dedicated virtual CPUs, corresponding to 72 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. The system is equipped with 1116.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 7.75 GB of memory per core.

This server includes 9383 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across 24 local SSDs, offering high-speed local data access without external network storage overhead. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, reaching 11519.35 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 533.92 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations perform exceptionally well, with PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations both ranking in the top 10%, the latter achieving 219.47 million operations per second.

The hardware configuration is optimized for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory data stores like Redis, and high-throughput static web serving. Additionally, the inclusion of Intel AMX instructions enables efficient CPU-based LLM inference, with prompt processing speeds for medium and large models ranking in the top 10% and top 25% respectively. While single-core performance is strong, the primary value of this instance lies in its massive parallel processing capabilities, high memory density, and local NVMe storage capacity, making it a cost-efficient choice for consolidated enterprise workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-144-lssd is a memory-optimized server designed for high-throughput, data-intensive workloads. It features 144 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz, alongside 1116.0 GB of RAM and 9383 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 24 drives. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and CPU-based LLM inference. Benchmark data highlights exceptional capabilities in database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 219.47 million operations per second. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, data warehousing, and demanding enterprise applications requiring high memory density and fast local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-144-lssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 144 vCPUs, 1116 GB RAM, and 9383 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-144-lssd features 144 vCPUs, 1116 GB RAM, and 9.3 TB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local NVMe SSDs for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 144 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors at 2.3 GHz.
1116.0 GB of RAM providing high memory density for large-scale applications.
9383 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 24 drives.
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in benchmark testing.
Intel AMX support accelerating CPU-based machine learning and LLM inference.
Optimized for in-memory databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving.
